pages.raw_text:
Three claim agents are busily en-
gagged in adjusting the claims for
damages growing out of the Occur
d'Alene wreck. The small claims
are being settled first and the
larger ones will then be taken up.
An official of the Inland Empire
system stated today that the COM
pany would gladly give a check for
$25O,000 II someone would take 1t
and agree tot adjust all the claims
for that amount.


The Graves company has no in
demnity insurance to cover it's
loss in the wreck 1t carries in
demnity insurance in the case of
its employees, but not on accidents
such as that at Occur d'Alene.


The heavy loss involved in the
wreck has hit the Inland a hard
blow and projects that were about
to be undertaken this year, like
the Big Bend road, have been
postponed.


The smallest claim so far set
tied was for so cents for the break
Ing of ferule off the end of an UNI
brella. The man making the claim
said that it was a present from his
wife and asked a dollar for the
damage sustained.


In another case a man and wom-
an presented claim for damaged
clothing and asked $3.50 for their
loss. This amount was paid.
